name: Floating Island
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, beat the egg whites until they begin to froth.
2. Gradually add 1/4 cup of sugar while continuing to beat the egg whites. Beat until stiff peaks form.
3. Scald the milk over medium heat. Remove from heat and stir in 1/4 cup of sugar and vanilla until dissolved.
4. Poach the remaining egg whites by creating small, loose bubbles on the surface of the hot milk. Cook for about 4 to 5 minutes, or until set. Gently lift with a spatula and place on a serving platter. These will form the "islands."
5. In another bowl, beat the egg yolks with the remaining sugar until pale.
6. Gradually add the hot milk to the yolk mixture, stirring vigorously to combine.
7. Allow the custard to cool slightly, then strain through a fine mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
8. To make caramel, combine 1/2 cup of sugar and 1/2 cup of water in a small saucepan over medium heat. Cook until the sugar melts and forms a syrup.
9. Serve the cooled milk-and-egg mixture with the "islands" and drizzle the caramel sauce on top.
Source: Great Chefs of New Orleans, Tele-record Productions - 1983
